top_signals.title: Japan Bonds Face Homegrown Downside Risks Even as Oil Retreats
top_signals.summary: A gauge of risk compensation for holding government bonds has risen fastest in Japan among major markets since the US-Iran war began, pointing to local factors that may continue to weigh on Japanese bonds even if energy prices retreat.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Emerging-Market Assets Rise as Oil Drops on US-Iran Deal Hopes
top_signals.summary: Emerging-market stocks and currencies rose as oil prices declined on signs that the US and Iran were nearing a deal that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
